description:
You are the backup administrator of a small server.  
A first batch of project data has already been placed for you under  

    /home/user/data/

containing two sub-directories:

    /home/user/data/projectA/
    /home/user/data/projectB/

Your assignment is to create a *performance-optimised* compressed
backup of **everything below `/home/user/data/`**, while making sure
that any file whose name ends in “.tmp” **or** “.bak” is completely
excluded from the archive.  
After that, gather a few runtime statistics and write them to a concise
log so that a monitoring system can later read the numbers.

Deliverables that **must exist once you have finished**:

1. Archive  
   Path: `/home/user/backups/data_backup.tar.gz`  
   Requirements:  
   • It has to contain **only** the non-excluded files that live below
     `/home/user/data/`.  
   • Directory structure inside the archive must start with `data/`
     (e.g. `data/projectA/report.txt`, not the absolute path).  
   • The archive has to be compressed with the fastest available
     parallel implementation you can find *first* (for example `pigz` or
     `pbzip2`).  
     If none of those programmes are present in the container, fall back
     to the standard single-threaded tool (`gzip`, `bzip2`, …) but use
     its highest compression level (e.g. `-9`).  
     The tool that was finally used is recorded in the log (see below).

2. Check-sum file  
   Path: `/home/user/backups/data_backup.tar.gz.sha256`  
   Format: exactly the single output line produced by  
           `sha256sum /home/user/backups/data_backup.tar.gz`  
   (i.e. the 64-character hexadecimal digest, two spaces, the full
   filename).

3. Log file  
   Path: `/home/user/backups/backup.log`  
   Format: **six** lines, each in the form KEY=VALUE (no surrounding
   spaces, capital letters as shown, one per line):

        START_EPOCH=<unix_epoch_integer>
        END_EPOCH=<unix_epoch_integer>
        DURATION_SEC=<integer>
        COMPRESSOR=<pigz|gzip|pbzip2|bzip2|xz|…>
        TOTAL_ARCHIVED_FILES=<integer>
        SIZE_BYTES=<integer>

   Precise rules:
   • `START_EPOCH` is the integer value of `date +%s` taken **immediately
     before** the archival command starts.  
   • `END_EPOCH` is the same but captured **immediately after** the
     archive is finished and flushed to disk.  
   • `DURATION_SEC` must be the arithmetic difference
     `END_EPOCH-START_EPOCH`.  
   • `COMPRESSOR` is the *programme name* actually used to compress
     (`pigz`, `gzip`, …).  
   • `TOTAL_ARCHIVED_FILES` is the exact number of regular files inside
     `data_backup.tar.gz` (the test suite will extract the file list with
     `tar -tzf` and count them).  
   • `SIZE_BYTES` is the byte size of `data_backup.tar.gz` as returned by
     `stat --format=%s`.

Naming, capitalisation and ordering of the keys must be **exactly** as
shown above so the automated checker can parse them.

Hints & boundaries:

• All work must be done from the unprivileged account that you already
  have; do not attempt to use `sudo`.  
• You are free to create helper files or scripts inside your home
  directory, but only the three deliverables mentioned above will be
  inspected.  
• Make sure the directory `/home/user/backups/` exists before you start
  writing files into it (create it if necessary).  
• To make the archive reproducible the monitoring team recommends
  passing options such as  
     `--sort=name --numeric-owner --owner=0 --group=0 --mtime='UTC 2023-01-01'`  
  to `tar`, but this is **optional**; only the deliverables matter.  
• Feel free to display console output while you work — that does not
  influence grading, only the presence and content of the three files do.

When everything is finished the automated test suite will:

1. Untar `data_backup.tar.gz` in memory and confirm that it contains
   exactly the three expected data files and no “.tmp” or “.bak” files.  
2. Verify that the number in `TOTAL_ARCHIVED_FILES` equals that count.  
3. Confirm that `END_EPOCH > START_EPOCH` and that
   `DURATION_SEC = END_EPOCH - START_EPOCH`.  
4. Recompute the SHA-256 digest of the archive and compare it to the
   contents of `data_backup.tar.gz.sha256`.  
5. Check that `SIZE_BYTES` matches the real file size returned by `stat`.

Accomplish the task using as few commands and as little wall-clock time
as reasonably possible while adhering to the required output formats.
truth:
Initial on-disk state before the agent starts:

1. Directories
   • /home/user/data/
   • /home/user/data/projectA/
   • /home/user/data/projectB/

2. Files and their **exact** byte content (final linefeeds included):

/home/user/data/projectA/report.txt
──────────────────────────────────
Annual Report 2023 final version
<LF>

/home/user/data/projectA/draft.tmp
──────────────────────────────────
temporary data
<LF>

/home/user/data/projectB/config.cfg
──────────────────────────────────
# sample configuration
version = 1.0
enabled = true
<LF>

/home/user/data/projectB/notes.txt
──────────────────────────────────
Meeting notes for upcoming release
Remember to update changelog.
<LF>

(“<LF>” denotes a single Unix newline character `\n`)

/home/user/backups/ **does not exist yet**.

Ground-truth expectations after successful completion:

A. /home/user/backups/data_backup.tar.gz
   • Must exist and be a valid gzip compressed tar file.
   • `tar -tzf` on it **must list exactly:**

        data/projectA/report.txt
        data/projectB/config.cfg
        data/projectB/notes.txt

     in any order (the test will sort before comparison).

B. /home/user/backups/data_backup.tar.gz.sha256
   • Must contain one single line:
        <64 hex chars><two spaces>/home/user/backups/data_backup.tar.gz
   • The 64-char digest must be the SHA-256 of the archive produced in A.

C. /home/user/backups/backup.log
   • Must exist and consist of exactly six non-empty lines in the
     order given in the public task description.
   • Keys must match *verbatim*.
   • `TOTAL_ARCHIVED_FILES` must be the integer 3.
   • `COMPRESSOR` must be one of: pigz, pbzip2, gzip, bzip2, xz
     (case-sensitive).  
   • `SIZE_BYTES` must equal the size of data_backup.tar.gz reported by
     `stat --format=%s /home/user/backups/data_backup.tar.gz`.

No other constraints are checked by the grading scripts.
